explicit_text: Buddhism has been an important part of my life, though I couldn't attend worship services often when my children were small. Recently, I've been able to attend regularly and feel undeservedly blessed when I listen to the teachings. I believe religion, especially Buddhism, instills morals in children which helps prevent them from doing bad things. In Japan, moral education was part of schooling for Issei, but in America, religion is what teaches us morals.
implicit_text: My spiritual practice has been an important part of my life, though I couldn't attend worship services often when my children were small. Recently, I've been able to attend regularly and feel undeservedly blessed when I listen to the teachings. I believe religion, especially the path founded by Siddhartha Gautama, instills morals in children which helps prevent them from doing bad things. In Japan, moral education was part of schooling for Issei, but in America, religion is what teaches us morals.

explicit_text: Priest Ishimoki once visited me and helped me by writing 'Keep this box' in neat English on a trunk full of my records. I had originally written 'Don't burn this' in Japanese because I couldn't write in English, though I could read it. His visit was very helpful in preserving important family documents.
implicit_text: A visiting priest once helped me by writing 'Keep this box' in neat English on a trunk full of my records. I had originally written 'Don't burn this' in Japanese because I couldn't write in English, though I could read it. His visit was very helpful in preserving important family documents.

explicit_text: Mrs. Tanaka and I have shared many conversations, especially when Reverend Takarabe visited me. She often comes by and we talk about various things, including serving manju and family matters. She is a kind person who helps make visits pleasant.
implicit_text: A kind woman and I have shared many conversations, especially when Reverend Takarabe visited me. She often comes by and we talk about various things, including serving manju and family matters. She is a kind person who helps make visits pleasant.

explicit_text: I was born in Japan and went back there to finish high school, returning to America during the Hoover Depression when I was 18. Many Japanese parents sent their children back to Japan for education, but this often caused a gap between parents and children. I have visited Japan several times, including once after receiving a telegram about my father's illness. I feel proud of my Japanese heritage and believe both Japanese in Japan and Japanese Americans should protect their traditions.
implicit_text: I was born in the country of the rising sun and went back there to finish high school, returning to America during the Hoover Depression when I was 18. Many parents of Japanese descent sent their children back to their ancestral homeland for education, but this often caused a gap between parents and children. I have visited that country several times, including once after receiving a telegram about my father's illness. I feel proud of my heritage and believe both those living in that country and Japanese Americans should protect their traditions.

explicit_text: I came back to America from Japan during the Hoover Depression, which was a very difficult time for everyone. I was 18 years old then, and the exclusionary movement against Japanese people was very strong. The economic hardship made life even tougher for us.
implicit_text: I came back to America from Japan during the early 1930s economic crisis, which was a very difficult time for everyone. I was 18 years old then, and the exclusionary movement against Japanese people was very strong. The economic hardship made life even tougher for us.

explicit_text: The Chronicle, which was Hearst's newspaper in San Francisco, was really bad to us Japanese people. It reflected the strong exclusionary mood that existed before and during World War II. The paper contributed to the difficult environment we faced in America at that time.
implicit_text: A major San Francisco newspaper owned by a prominent media tycoon was really bad to us Japanese people. It reflected the strong exclusionary mood that existed before and during World War II. The paper contributed to the difficult environment we faced in America at that time.

explicit_text: Hearst Corporation owned The Chronicle newspaper in San Francisco, which was very hostile toward Japanese Americans before and during the war. Their coverage contributed to the racial tensions and exclusionary attitudes we experienced. It was a challenging time for our community.
implicit_text: The media corporation owned the San Francisco newspaper that was very hostile toward Japanese Americans before and during the war. Their coverage contributed to the racial tensions and exclusionary attitudes we experienced. It was a challenging time for our community.

explicit_text: World War II was a racial war, and it was very hard on Japanese people both in Japan and America. Although it was bad that Japan lost, it turned out good in some ways because after the war, many Japanese Americans found employment with the State, which was rare before. The war changed a lot about how Japanese Americans were treated.
implicit_text: The global conflict from 1939 to 1945 was a racial war, and it was very hard on Japanese people both in Japan and America. Although it was bad that Japan lost, it turned out good in some ways because after the war, many Japanese Americans found employment with the State, which was rare before. The war changed a lot about how Japanese Americans were treated.

explicit_text: I have a passport issued by the American Consulate in Kobe, Japan, dated January 14, 1918. It shows that I, Mitsuyo Sakada, a Japanese subject, was traveling to the United States with my daughter under 20 years old. This document is an important part of my family's history and our migration story.

It shows that I, Mitsuyo Sakada, a Japanese subject, was traveling to the United States with my daughter under 20 years old. This document is an important part of my family's history and our migration story.

explicit_text: I am Mitsuyo Sakada, a housewife originally from Japan. I traveled to the United States with my daughters, including Sumiko and Yoshiko, during difficult times. I have many memories tied to my family and our experiences moving between Japan and America, especially during the early 20th century.
implicit_text: I am a housewife originally from Japan. I traveled to the United States with my daughters, including Sumiko and Yoshiko, during difficult times. I have many memories tied to my family and our experiences moving between Japan and America, especially during the early 20th century.

explicit_text: Sumiko Sakada, my daughter, was born in Oakland, California, on June 16, 1916. She often comes to see me, and I took her with me when I went back to Japan after receiving a telegram about my father's critical condition. She was still very young then, even wearing diapers on the boat trip to Yokohama.
implicit_text: My daughter, born in Oakland, California, on June 16, 1916, often comes to see me. I took her with me when I went back to Japan after receiving a telegram about my father's critical condition. She was still very young then, even wearing diapers on the boat trip to Yokohama.

explicit_text: My daughter Sumiko was born in Oakland, California, in 1916. Oakland is where part of our family roots lie in America. It was a place connected to our early life before traveling back and forth to Japan.
implicit_text: My daughter was born in a city in the San Francisco Bay Area in 1916. That city is where part of our family roots lie in America. It was a place connected to our early life before traveling back and forth to Japan.

explicit_text: When I returned to Japan with my daughters, the boat trip to Yokohama took only two days. I remember throwing away Sumiko's diapers into the sea during the voyage. Yokohama was the port where we arrived and marked the beginning of our visit back to Japan after many years.
implicit_text: When I returned to Japan with my daughters, the boat trip to the port city near Tokyo took only two days. I remember throwing away Sumiko's diapers into the sea during the voyage. That port was where we arrived and marked the beginning of our visit back to Japan after many years.

explicit_text: Yoshiko Oshima is my eldest daughter who married Mr. Oshima. She accompanied me on my trip back to Japan when my father was ill. She often visits me here, and I cherish her presence in our family gatherings.
implicit_text: My eldest daughter married Mr. Oshima. She accompanied me on my trip back to Japan when my father was ill. She often visits me here, and I cherish her presence in our family gatherings.

explicit_text: My daughter Kimiko married into the Miyamoto family. The Miyamotos are part of our extended family, and we keep close ties with them. Family connections like these are very important to us.
implicit_text: My daughter Kimiko married into the Miyamoto family. The Miyamotos are part of our extended family, and we keep close ties with them. Family connections like these are very important to us.

explicit_text: I once visited the grandmother of Nijōji, who was very good at writing and kept detailed records about her time in the camp. She wrote for a newspaper and preserved many memories that help us understand our history. Her dedication to recording the past is admirable.
implicit_text: I once visited the grandmother of a young man named Nijōji, who was very good at writing and kept detailed records about her time in the camp. She wrote for a newspaper and preserved many memories that help us understand our history. Her dedication to recording the past is admirable.

explicit_text: After leaving the camp, some people, like Mrs. Miyasaki, stayed in a hotel in Fresno for a while before returning to Sacramento. Fresno was a place where many Japanese Americans lived and passed through during and after the war. It was a community hub for us.
implicit_text: After leaving the camp, some people, like Mrs. Miyasaki, stayed in a hotel in a Central California city for a while before returning to Sacramento. That city was a place where many Japanese Americans lived and passed through during and after the war. It was a community hub for us.

explicit_text: My husband was born in Sacramento, and after the war, we decided to stay here rather than move back to Japan. Sacramento became our home again, and we built our life here. It holds a special place in our family's story.
implicit_text: My husband was born in the capital of California, and after the war, we decided to stay here rather than move back to Japan. That city became our home again, and we built our life here. It holds a special place in our family's story.

explicit_text: There was a commanding officer named Webster during the camp period. He made a harsh comment calling us 'Jap,' which angered my husband. This moment showed the tension and difficulty we faced, but also the courage of my husband who affirmed his loyalty to America despite the insults.
implicit_text: There was a commanding officer named Webster during the camp period. He made a harsh comment calling us 'Jap,' which angered my husband. This moment showed the tension and difficulty we faced, but also the courage of my husband who affirmed his loyalty to America despite the insults.

explicit_text: My husband was a true-born Nisei and couldn't write Japanese at all because they didn't teach it in America then. The Nisei generation faced unique challenges, caught between two cultures. Despite this, many remained loyal to America, even during difficult times like the war and internment.
implicit_text: My husband was a true-born second-generation Japanese American and couldn't write Japanese at all because they didn't teach it in America then. That generation faced unique challenges, caught between two cultures. Despite this, many remained loyal to America, even during difficult times like the war and internment.

explicit_text: My husband was sent to Gerome after answering questions about allegiance during the camp period. It was part of the internment system, and life there was tough. We all had to endure many hardships during that time.
implicit_text: My husband was sent to a segregation center after answering questions about allegiance during the camp period. It was part of the internment system, and life there was tough. We all had to endure many hardships during that time.

explicit_text: Sansei, the third generation, often do not know much about the Issei and their culture or hardships. I wish they could understand and be proud of their Japanese heritage. It is important for them to remember where they come from and keep our traditions alive.
implicit_text: The third generation often do not know much about the first generation and their culture or hardships. I wish they could understand and be proud of their Japanese heritage. It is important for them to remember where they come from and keep our traditions alive.

explicit_text: Yonsei, the fourth generation, like the Sansei, often lack knowledge about the Issei's struggles and culture. I hope they will learn to appreciate their roots and maintain the traditional ethics of being Japanese. It is vital for our community's identity.
implicit_text: The fourth generation, like the third, often lack knowledge about the first generation's struggles and culture. I hope they will learn to appreciate their roots and maintain the traditional ethics of being Japanese. It is vital for our community's identity.

explicit_text: The Issei, the first generation, were taught morals and ethics in Japan which they tried to pass on to their children here. They faced many hardships and discrimination but remained proud of their Japanese identity. I believe it is important for younger generations to understand and respect the Issei's experiences.
implicit_text: The first generation were taught morals and ethics in Japan which they tried to pass on to their children here. They faced many hardships and discrimination but remained proud of their Japanese identity. I believe it is important for younger generations to understand and respect the first generation's experiences.

explicit_text: Mr. Kinya Noguchi, a Japanese war son, believed that people of Japanese descent should unite regardless of nationality to better integrate with America. I heard him say that unity among different races is important for building this country. His perspective was one way to approach our identity in America.
implicit_text: A Japanese war son named Kinya believed that people of Japanese descent should unite regardless of nationality to better integrate with America. I heard him say that unity among different races is important for building this country. His perspective was one way to approach our identity in America.

explicit_text: I heard it proudly said that Brazil will soon have a President of Japanese descent. This shows how Japanese people have spread and succeeded in many countries. It is inspiring to see our heritage recognized globally.
implicit_text: I heard it proudly said that a South American country will soon have a President of Japanese descent. This shows how Japanese people have spread and succeeded in many countries. It is inspiring to see our heritage recognized globally.

explicit_text: I am from Kagoshima, and I know people like Tsuchimoshi Kaikyoshi who are also from there. Kagoshima is our hometown in Japan, and it connects us to our roots and traditions. It is a place I remember fondly.
implicit_text: I am from a southern prefecture on Kyushu island, and I know people like Tsuchimoshi Kaikyoshi who are also from there. That place is our hometown in Japan, and it connects us to our roots and traditions. It is a place I remember fondly.

explicit_text: I know Tsuchimoshi Kaikyoshi, who is also from Kagoshima like me. Knowing people from my hometown gives me a sense of connection and belonging, even while living far away in America.
implicit_text: I know Tsuchimoshi Kaikyoshi, who is also from my southern Kyushu hometown. Knowing people from my hometown gives me a sense of connection and belonging, even while living far away in America.

explicit_text: During my time at Tule Lake War Relocation Center, I washed dishes and practiced shamisen. I regret not learning English there, but I did my best to keep busy. Life in the camp was hard, but some people even missed it because food was secured for families with many children.
implicit_text: During my time at the segregation center in Northern California, I washed dishes and practiced shamisen. I regret not learning English there, but I did my best to keep busy. Life in the camp was hard, but some people even missed it because food was secured for families with many children.

explicit_text: There was a man from San Jose who worked in the kitchen at Tule Lake with me. I heard he has since passed away. San Jose is part of the wider Japanese American community connected through these camps and shared experiences.
implicit_text: There was a man from a city in the South Bay of San Francisco who worked in the kitchen at that Northern California camp with me. I heard he has since passed away. That city is part of the wider Japanese American community connected through these camps and shared experiences.

explicit_text: Mr. and Mrs. Itano are a happy couple who stayed together through everything, and their son is a steady man. I admire their strength and stability, which is a model for our community. They represent the resilience of Japanese Americans.
implicit_text: Mr. and Mrs. Itano are a happy couple who stayed together through everything, and their son is a steady man. I admire their strength and stability, which is a model for our community. They represent the resilience of Japanese Americans.

explicit_text: My daughter is now teaching at a college after graduating from the University of California. Sometimes I feel she thinks differently from me because she was born and raised in America. The University of California represents the opportunities available to younger generations.
implicit_text: My daughter is now teaching at a college after graduating from a major public university in California. Sometimes I feel she thinks differently from me because she was born and raised in America. That university represents the opportunities available to younger generations.
